title: Fully Funded Trinity Research Doctorate Award 2026–2030 in Nature-Based Solutions for Catchment Management in Ireland
summary: Are you passionate about environmental history, climate adaptation, landscape change, GIS, or community-based environmental research? Trinity College Dublin is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD studentship under the prestigious Trinity Research Doctorate Award (TRDA) 2026–2030 . This exciting doctoral opportunity focuses on Nature-Based Solutions for Catchment Management in Ireland and offers scholars the chance to contribute to cutting-edge interdisciplinary climate research while receiving full financial support. The PhD forms part of the ambitious Landscape Living Lab: Catchment Monitoring, Modelling, and Management project, funded through the AIB Trinity Climate Hub Trinity Research Doctorate Award Scheme . The successful candidate will work alongside leading academics and fellow PhD researchers to help shape innovative approaches to climate resilience, flood risk management, and sustainable landscape governance in Ireland. Application Deadline: Monday, 20 July 2026 About the Trinity Research Doctorate Award The Trinity Research Doctorate Award (TRDA) is one of Trinity College Dublin s flagship doctoral funding schemes that supports outstanding interdisciplinary research tackling global challenges. This project is developed under the theme of Nature-Based Solutions , bringing together expertise from environmental humanities, geography, civil engineering, environmental science, business, and climate research. The successful PhD candidate will become part of a collaborative research cohort examining the complex interactions between environmental systems and human societies within a river catchment in County Wicklow, Ireland . About the Landscape Living Lab Project The PhD is one component of the larger interdisciplinary research programme titled: Landscape Living Lab: Catchment Monitoring, Modelling, and Management The project investigates how river catchments can become more resilient to climate change through integrated research across four interconnected research pillars known as the 4Ms : Monitoring Current environmental dynamics Climate-related risks Nature-related risks Modelling Future resilience pathways Climate adaptation scenarios Predictive environmental modelling Memory Historical landscape change Environmental history Community knowledge Historical vulnerability Management Decision-making frameworks Nature-based solutions Sustainable catchment governance Each PhD candidate will specialize in one pillar while collaborating closely with the others. About This PhD Project The advertised studentship focuses on the Memory pillar under the title: Reconstructing Catchment Memory: Environmental History, Landscape Change and Community Knowledge for Nature-Based Catchment Management in an Irish Living Lab The project will be supervised by: Associate Professor Francis Ludlow School of Histories and Humanities Trinity College Dublin Research Objectives The research aims to reconstruct the environmental and social history of a river catchment in County Wicklow. The student will investigate how landscapes have changed over time and how these changes influence today s environmental challenges, particularly flooding and climate resilience. The research combines multiple disciplines including: Environmental history Historical GIS Digital humanities Art history Oral history Landscape studies Climate adaptation Nature-based solutions What Will the PhD Research Involve? The successful researcher will examine a wide variety of historical and environmental sources, including: Historical estate records Historical maps Aerial photography Paintings Photographs Forestry documentation Drainage records Local government archives Rural district council documents Historical newspaper reports Flood records Landscape change documentation These materials will be digitized and integrated into a Geographic Information System (GIS) to reconstruct historical environmental conditions. Community Engagement and Participatory Mapping An important feature of the project is collaboration with local communities. The PhD researcher will work directly with: Local residents Farmers Landholders Community organizations Catchment stakeholders Using ethical participatory methods, the researcher will collect community knowledge about: Historical woodland cover Wetland loss River changes Flood-prone areas Historic drainage systems Field boundaries Land improvement projects Infrastructure development Community mental maps will then be compared with documentary evidence to understand both environmental change and collective memory. Key Research Questions The project seeks answers to several important questions, including: How have land use and land cover changed across the catchment? When did significant environmental changes occur? Which historical management decisions contributed to today s flood risks? How can historical records be integrated into GIS-based environmental analysis? How can oral histories improve understanding of environmental change? How can historical evidence improve future nature-based catchment management? Candidate Profile Applicants should ideally possess: At least an Upper Second-Class Honours Bachelor s degree A Master s degree or equivalent experience is highly desirable Relevant academic backgrounds include: Environmental History Historical GIS Geography Digital Humanities Landscape Studies Archaeology Anthropology Art History Environmental Science Related disciplines Desired Skills and Experience Applicants should demonstrate experience or strong interest in: Historical archive research Documentary analysis Historical map interpretation Visual source analysis GIS software (QGIS, ArcGIS or equivalent) Digital mapping Spatial databases Environmental history Climate adaptation Nature-based solutions Flood risk studies Oral history Community engagement Participatory mapping Qualitative research methods Interdisciplinary research Academic writing Public communication The successful candidate should also be comfortable engaging respectfully with local communities and collaborating across disciplines. Funding Benefits This is a fully funded four-year PhD scholarship that includes: Annual tax-free stipend of €25,000 Full tuition fee coverage Four years of funding Opportunity to work within a major interdisciplinary climate research programme Collaboration with leading researchers at Trinity College Dublin Training in advanced interdisciplinary research methods Participation in the AIB Trinity Climate Hub Application Process Applicants should email their application to: Associate Professor Francis Ludlow Subject Line: Trinity College PhD Application: Catchment Memory Applications should include: Curriculum Vitae (CV) 1–2 page personal statement The personal statement should explain: Why you are interested in this PhD Why you are a strong candidate Relevant experience Research interests Applicants should also include contact information for two referees in their CV. Apply to fludlow@tcd.ie Selection Process Following submission: Applications will be shortlisted. Interviews are expected in late July 2026 . The successful applicant will then submit a formal PhD application through Trinity College Dublin s School of Histories and Humanities. The successful candidate will begin the PhD on 1 September 2026 . Important Dates Application Deadline: Monday, 20 July 2026 Expected Interviews: Late July 2026 Programme Start Date: 1 September 2026 Why You Should Apply This fully funded PhD offers an outstanding opportunity to contribute to internationally significant research addressing climate resilience and sustainable landscape management. By combining environmental history, digital mapping, community engagement, and nature-based solutions, the project will help shape future approaches to flood management and environmental governance in Ireland. Successful applicants will join an interdisciplinary research environment at one of Europe s leading universities while gaining advanced research skills applicable across academia, environmental consultancy, policy development, GIS, and climate adaptation. For more information on this scholarship opportunity visit here For more scholarship opportunities on OFY, visit here The post Fully Funded Trinity Research Doctorate Award 2026–2030 in Nature-Based Solutions for Catchment Management in Ireland appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Reckitt Catalyst Global Accelerator 2026: Funding, Mentorship & Global Support for Health and Hygiene Entrepreneurs
summary: Entrepreneurs developing innovative solutions to improve healthcare, water, sanitation, and hygiene now have an exciting opportunity to accelerate the growth of their ventures through the Reckitt Catalyst Global Accelerator . The globally recognized initiative is currently accepting applications from impact-driven startups operating across selected countries in Africa, Asia, and Latin America. Delivered in partnership with Yunus Social Innovation (YSI) , Health Innovation Exchange (HIEx) , and Acumen America , the Reckitt Catalyst program supports entrepreneurs creating practical, scalable, and community-centered innovations that expand access to health and hygiene for underserved populations. Since its launch, the program has helped more than 80 social innovators across 15 countries , reaching over 2 million people with life-saving health and hygiene solutions. Reckitt has also committed to supporting up to 200 female and underrepresented founders by 2030 , with the ambition of impacting up to 5 million people through improved access to health and hygiene. About the Reckitt Catalyst Global Accelerator Reckitt believes that health begins at home. Through its Stronger Communities initiative, the company invests in entrepreneurs who understand the communities they serve and are building innovative solutions to address pressing health and sanitation challenges. Rather than focusing only on financial support, Reckitt Catalyst provides founders with an ecosystem that combines: Funding Expert mentorship Strategic partnerships Business development support Industry expertise Global visibility Access to networks and investors The program aims to help entrepreneurs scale innovations that strengthen healthcare systems, improve hygiene practices, and increase access to safe water and sanitation. Who Can Apply? Applications are open to entrepreneurs operating in: Nigeria South Africa Kenya India Pakistan Indonesia Mexico Brazil Applications from entrepreneurs in the United States are accepted throughout the year on a rolling basis. The accelerator welcomes ventures developing scalable innovations that improve: Healthcare access Water access Sanitation Hygiene Public health systems Community resilience What the Program Offers Successful entrepreneurs receive comprehensive support designed to help them grow sustainable and scalable businesses. Benefits include: Funding Support Selected ventures receive financial support to expand their impact and accelerate growth. World-Class Mentorship Entrepreneurs gain direct access to experienced business leaders, technical experts, and industry professionals who provide strategic guidance throughout the accelerator. Business Expertise Participants receive practical support in areas such as: Business strategy Product development Market expansion Partnerships Investment readiness Scaling operations Global Network The program connects entrepreneurs with: Investors Government partners Healthcare experts Innovation ecosystems Corporate leaders Fellow entrepreneurs Visibility Participants gain international exposure through Reckitt s global platform, helping increase credibility and open doors to additional partnerships and investment opportunities. Program Partners The accelerator is delivered alongside globally recognized organizations: Yunus Social Innovation (YSI) Health Innovation Exchange (HIEx) Acumen America These partners contribute technical expertise, mentorship, innovation support, and investment readiness resources. Focus Areas The accelerator supports innovations addressing essential health and hygiene challenges, including: Healthcare Solutions improving access to healthcare services and health outcomes. Examples include: Digital health Telemedicine AI-powered healthcare Emergency response Women s health Medical supply chains Pharmacy technology Water Innovations expanding access to clean and safe drinking water. Examples include: Water purification Atmospheric water generation Rainwater harvesting Smart water infrastructure Water treatment technologies Sanitation Solutions improving sanitation infrastructure and public health. Examples include: Wastewater treatment Sanitation technologies Toilets and hygiene systems Circular sanitation models Hygiene Innovations promoting healthier communities through better hygiene practices. Examples include: Hygiene education Behavioral change programs Community hygiene technologies Household hygiene innovations Impact Achieved So Far Reckitt Catalyst has already demonstrated significant global impact. Program achievements include: Supporting more than 80 social innovators Operating across 15 countries Reaching over 2 million people Five-year commitment to support up to 200 female and underrepresented founders Goal of improving health and hygiene access for up to 5 million people by 2030 Entrepreneur-in-Residence Reckitt Catalyst has partnered with Serena Williams as Entrepreneur-in-Residence. As an entrepreneur, investor, and advocate for equity, Serena Williams mentors participating founders while helping amplify their innovations globally. Examples of Supported Ventures The accelerator has already supported numerous impactful enterprises across Africa, Asia, and the Americas. Examples include: Africa Bulamu Bridge AI (Uganda) Débbo Africa Emergency Response Africa (Nigeria) LifeBank Zoie Health Aqua Clara Solutions Iriba Water Group Asia EveryWater (Pakistan) Sehat Kahani Banka BioLoo Solinas Komodo Water Latin America Zócalo Health Isla Urbana O2Eco Global Health Innovation Attane Health Ema ThriveLink The Washing Machine Project These ventures demonstrate the accelerator s commitment to supporting scalable, technology-driven solutions addressing health and hygiene challenges worldwide. Why Apply? Participating entrepreneurs can benefit from: Funding to scale operations Personalized mentorship Global recognition Corporate partnerships Business growth support International networking opportunities Investment readiness Access to Reckitt s global innovation ecosystem Increased visibility among investors and partners Application Information Applications are currently open. Eligible entrepreneurs should submit their applications through the Reckitt Catalyst application portal. Applications from entrepreneurs based in the United States are reviewed on a rolling basis. Applicants should ensure they clearly demonstrate: The health or hygiene challenge they are addressing Their innovative solution Evidence of impact Scalability Community engagement Long-term sustainability Apply Here Why Reckitt Catalyst Stands Out Unlike many startup accelerators, Reckitt Catalyst focuses specifically on community-led innovations that improve health, hygiene, sanitation, and water access. The program combines funding with long-term mentorship, strategic partnerships, and access to a global network, enabling entrepreneurs to scale meaningful solutions that improve lives while building sustainable businesses. Whether developing AI-powered healthcare, clean water technologies, women s health platforms, sanitation systems, or digital public health innovations, entrepreneurs have an opportunity to join a global movement working toward healthier and more resilient communities. For more information on this opportunity, visit here Discover more global opportunities on OFY, here The post Reckitt Catalyst Global Accelerator 2026: Funding, Mentorship Global Support for Health and Hygiene Entrepreneurs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Allianz Climate Risk Award 2026: Global Opportunity for PhD Candidates and Postdoctoral Researchers Working on Climate Change and Extreme Weather
summary: Climate change continues to reshape economies, societies, and ecosystems around the world, making innovative research on climate resilience more important than ever. To recognize and promote groundbreaking scientific research in this field, Allianz Re has officially launched applications for the Allianz Climate Risk Award 2026 . Celebrating its 10th anniversary , the Allianz Climate Risk Award continues its mission of connecting emerging climate scientists with the global insurance industry, helping translate scientific discoveries into practical solutions that strengthen resilience against climate-related risks. Researchers whose work focuses on climate change, extreme weather events, technological innovation, and resilience are encouraged to apply for this prestigious international award. Application Deadline Deadline: Applications should be submitted before the official closing date specified by Allianz for the 2026 Award cycle. Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible. About the Allianz Climate Risk Award Established in 2017 , the Allianz Climate Risk Award is Allianz Re s flagship annual scientific award dedicated to supporting early-career researchers investigating climate-related risks. The award recognizes outstanding scientific work that helps answer important questions such as: How is climate change intensifying extreme weather events? How can societies better prepare for future climate risks? Which innovative technologies can improve resilience? How can scientific research help governments, insurers, businesses, and communities reduce disaster risks? The award serves as an important bridge between academic research and practical implementation by connecting promising researchers with experts from the insurance and risk management sectors. Celebrating 10 Years of Climate Research Excellence The 2026 edition marks a major milestone—the 10th anniversary of the Allianz Climate Risk Award. Over the past decade, the programme has supported numerous young researchers whose work has contributed significantly to improving understanding of climate risks and resilience worldwide. To expand its impact, the programme now operates across three award tracks : Global Award Thematic Spotlight: Allianz Trade Regional Spotlight: Asia (new for 2026) These tracks allow the programme to recognize excellence across different geographical and thematic areas while maintaining its commitment to advancing climate science. Research Areas Supported Applicants should be conducting research related to one or both of the following areas: 1. Understanding and Mitigating Climate Risks Research that investigates: Climate change impacts Extreme weather events Floods Droughts Hurricanes Heatwaves Wildfires Climate modelling Disaster risk reduction Adaptation strategies Risk assessment 2. Building Climate Resilience Through Technology Research involving: Artificial Intelligence Remote sensing Climate modelling technologies Early warning systems Digital resilience tools Predictive analytics Climate data science Infrastructure resilience Nature-based technological solutions Innovative adaptation technologies Projects that combine scientific excellence with practical application are especially encouraged. Who Can Apply? The Allianz Climate Risk Award welcomes applications from: PhD candidates Postdoctoral researchers Applicants should be conducting research focused on climate risks, climate adaptation, resilience, or technological innovations that address climate-related challenges. The programme is international, welcoming applications from researchers around the world. Why Apply? Successful applicants gain numerous professional advantages, including: International recognition Visibility within the global scientific community Exposure to leading experts in insurance and climate risk Networking opportunities Career advancement Opportunity to showcase innovative research Stronger connections between academia and industry Contribution to global climate resilience efforts Application Requirements Applicants must prepare several documents as part of their submission. These include: Completed application forms Signed documentation Curriculum Vitae (CV) Reference letter Supporting documents Non-technical research essay Non-Technical Research Essay The central part of the application is a concise non-technical essay . Requirements include: Maximum 800 words Clearly explain your research Describe your research objectives Highlight key findings Explain the real-world significance of your work Use language understandable to non-specialists Applicants are encouraged to review previous winning essays published in earlier Allianz Climate Risk Award Compendiums for inspiration. Previous Award Compendiums Applicants can explore previous award-winning research through Allianz s published compendiums, including: Pathways to Resilience: Mitigation and Adaptation in a Changing Climate (2024) Resilience in Action (2023) The Era of Resilience (2022) Confronting Climate Change (2021) A Glimpse of Tomorrow (2020) The End of Normal (2019) Weathering the Storms (2018) The Allianz Climate Risk Research Award (2017) These publications showcase high-quality research and offer valuable guidance for prospective applicants. How to Apply Interested researchers should submit the following by email: Completed application forms Signed documents Non-technical essay CV Reference letter Required supporting documents Applications should be sent to: climaterisk-award@allianz.com Applicants should carefully review the official application guidelines before submitting. Why This Award Matters Climate change remains one of the defining global challenges of the 21st century. Scientific research plays a critical role in helping governments, insurers, businesses, and communities better understand and respond to increasing climate risks. The Allianz Climate Risk Award recognizes researchers who are developing innovative knowledge and practical solutions capable of improving resilience against increasingly frequent and severe weather events. For early-career scientists, it offers an exceptional platform to gain international recognition, engage with leading experts, and contribute to shaping a more resilient future. For more information on this opportunity, visit here Discover more global opportunities on OFY, here The post Allianz Climate Risk Award 2026: Global Opportunity for PhD Candidates and Postdoctoral Researchers Working on Climate Change and Extreme Weather appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: African Union CIEFFA Online Capacity Building Training 2026: Free Digital Skills, STEAM, Financial Literacy and Entrepreneurship Training for Africans Worldwide
summary: The African Union International Centre for Girls’ and Women’s Education in Africa (AU CIEFFA) , in partnership with ImpactHER , has officially opened applications for the African Union CIEFFA Online Capacity Building Training 2026 under the theme “#AfricaEducatesHer – Digital Skills for All: Bridging the Gender Gap in STEAM, Financial Literacy, and Entrepreneurship.” This fully sponsored online training programme is designed to equip women and men from all 55 African Union Member States and members of the African Diaspora with practical knowledge and skills in gender equality, STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts and Mathematics), digital literacy, financial literacy, and entrepreneurship . The initiative aims to empower participants to become leaders and change-makers who contribute to inclusive education, economic empowerment, and sustainable development across Africa. The programme, which begins in August 2026 , builds on the success of previous editions that have already certified more than 12,500 young people across 55 African countries and 34 non-African countries , making it one of the continent’s leading online capacity-building initiatives. About the African Union CIEFFA Online Capacity Building Training The African Union CIEFFA Online Capacity Building Training is a regional initiative jointly implemented by the African Union CIEFFA and ImpactHER to strengthen the capacity of African youth through accessible online learning. The programme recognizes that despite growing opportunities in digital transformation and entrepreneurship, many young Africans—particularly women—continue to face barriers to accessing quality education, digital skills, financial knowledge, and entrepreneurial opportunities. By combining practical online learning with gender-responsive education, the programme seeks to prepare participants to thrive in today s rapidly evolving digital economy. The 2026 edition focuses on developing skills that are increasingly essential for employment, innovation, business creation, and community development across Africa. About African Union CIEFFA The African Union International Centre for Girls’ and Women’s Education in Africa (AU CIEFFA) is a specialized institution of the African Union operating under the Department of Education, Science, Technology and Innovation (ESTI) . The Centre was established to promote: Gender equality in education Girls and women s education Skills development Educational advocacy Inclusive education across Africa Its work supports the African Union s broader education and development agenda while promoting equal educational opportunities for women and girls. About ImpactHER ImpactHER is an internationally recognized non-profit organization committed to empowering African women entrepreneurs. Its mission focuses on: Bridging the gender financing gap Supporting women-owned businesses Providing entrepreneurial education Promoting financial inclusion Creating economic opportunities for African women By partnering with AU CIEFFA, ImpactHER brings practical entrepreneurship and financial literacy expertise to participants across the continent. Theme for the 2026 Training The 2026 programme will be delivered under the theme: #AfricaEducatesHer – Digital Skills for All: Bridging the Gender Gap in STEAM, Financial Literacy, and Entrepreneurship. The theme emphasizes the importance of ensuring equal access to digital knowledge and entrepreneurial opportunities while promoting gender equality in education throughout Africa. Objectives of the Programme The online training seeks to: Strengthen digital skills among African youth. Promote gender equality in and through education. Increase access to STEAM education. Improve financial literacy. Develop entrepreneurial skills. Empower young Africans to become community leaders. Promote inclusive economic growth. Encourage innovation and digital transformation across Africa. Training Areas Participants will receive training in several key areas, including: 1. Gender Equality in Education Participants will learn how gender-responsive education contributes to inclusive learning environments and sustainable development. Topics include: Gender equality in education Inclusive educational practices Women s empowerment Educational leadership 2. STEAM Education The programme promotes access to STEAM by helping participants understand how science, technology, engineering, arts, and mathematics contribute to solving real-world problems. 3. Digital Skills Participants will strengthen practical digital competencies needed in today s workforce and entrepreneurial ecosystem. Learning areas include: Digital literacy Technology applications Online productivity Digital innovation 4. Financial Literacy The training equips participants with knowledge in: Personal finance Budgeting Financial planning Savings Business finance 5. Entrepreneurship Participants will learn practical entrepreneurial skills, including: Business development Opportunity identification Business planning Innovation Enterprise management Benefits of the Programme Successful participants will receive numerous benefits, including: Free Online Training The programme is delivered online, allowing participants to learn from anywhere with internet access. Certificates Participants who attend the required percentage of each programme will receive certificates in: Digital Skills Entrepreneurial Skills Financial Literacy According to the application information, participants are expected to attend the required proportion of sessions for each programme to qualify for certification. Join Prestigious Networks Participants become members of: African Union CIEFFA Network ImpactHER Network These networks provide opportunities for collaboration, learning, and future engagement. Opportunities for Future Fully Funded Activities Completing the programme may increase participants chances of being invited to fully funded in-person activities organized by African Union CIEFFA. Access to Additional Training Participants may also gain access to future fully funded online training programmes sponsored by ImpactHER. Programme Format The programme will be delivered entirely online. Selected applicants will be divided into different batches. Each batch will undergo: One month of training Online learning sessions Practical skills development Training begins on 31 August 2026 . Who Can Apply? Applications are open to: African women African men African youth Members of the African Diaspora Applicants must demonstrate an interest in building skills in education, entrepreneurship, digital technologies, and leadership. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be an African woman or man. Hold African citizenship. Reside in Africa or anywhere in the world. Demonstrate motivation to learn digital skills, entrepreneurial skills, and financial literacy. Show leadership potential and commitment to promoting girls and women s education. Have access to a reliable internet connection. Have access to a laptop, computer, smartphone, or tablet. Understand at least one programme language: English French Portuguese Why You Should Apply This programme offers much more than online learning. Participants will have the opportunity to: Develop in-demand digital skills. Improve entrepreneurial capacity. Gain financial literacy. Join continental professional networks. Earn recognized certificates. Build leadership skills. Access future African Union opportunities. Contribute to gender equality and inclusive development in Africa. For young Africans seeking practical, career-enhancing skills while joining a continent-wide network of innovators and leaders, this training presents an outstanding opportunity. Application Process Interested applicants should complete the official online application form. Applicants should ensure they: Select their preferred language (English, French, or Portuguese). Complete all required application fields. Submit the application before the closing date. Applicants with questions may contact: youth.cieffa@gmail.com hello@impacther.org APPLY HERE Application Deadline 31 July 2026 Late applications may not be considered, so interested applicants are encouraged to apply early. Click HERE to learn more about the African Union CIEFFA Online Capacity Building Training 2026. Conclusion The African Union CIEFFA Online Capacity Building Training 2026 represents an exceptional opportunity for Africans across the continent and in the diaspora to strengthen their knowledge in digital skills, STEAM, entrepreneurship, financial literacy, and gender-responsive education. With free online learning, recognized certificates, access to continental networks, and opportunities to participate in future fully funded African Union activities, the programme is well suited for aspiring entrepreneurs, students, professionals, educators, and community leaders seeking to contribute to Africa s digital and economic transformation. Eligible applicants should prepare their applications and submit them before 31 July 2026 to take advantage of this valuable learning opportunity. Discover more global African and Online Courses opportunities on OFY. title: Fully Funded SAIPA Scholarship Programme 2026: Study BCom Accounting in South Africa with Full Tuition, Accommodation, Books, and Monthly Allowance
summary: The South African Institute of Professional Accountants (SAIPA) has officially opened applications for its Fully Funded SAIPA Scholarship Programme 2026 , offering aspiring accountants the opportunity to pursue a BCom Accounting degree (or equivalent) at a recognized public university in South Africa. Designed to support academically talented students from financially disadvantaged backgrounds, the scholarship removes major financial barriers to higher education by covering tuition fees, accommodation, textbooks, and providing a monthly living allowance. If you dream of becoming a professional accountant and building a successful career in South Africa s accounting and finance sector, this scholarship presents an outstanding opportunity to receive comprehensive financial support while studying at university. About SAIPA The South African Institute of Professional Accountants (SAIPA) is one of South Africa s leading professional accountancy bodies, dedicated to developing highly skilled accounting professionals. With nearly four decades of experience and a membership exceeding 15,000 professional accountants , SAIPA has built a strong reputation for advancing the accounting profession through modern education, professional training, ethical leadership, and technology-driven learning. The SAIPA Scholarship Programme reflects the institute s commitment to expanding access to quality accounting education while strengthening the future of the profession in South Africa. About the SAIPA Scholarship Programme For the 2026 academic year , SAIPA will award five fully funded scholarships to deserving students pursuing a Bachelor of Commerce (BCom) in Accounting or an equivalent qualification. Among these awards is the Rashied Small Memorial Scholarship , established to honor the late Professor Rashied Small , a respected leader in South Africa s accounting profession who championed equitable access to accounting education. This memorial scholarship celebrates his enduring legacy by helping talented students achieve their educational and professional aspirations. What the Scholarship Covers The SAIPA Scholarship is a comprehensive funding package designed to support students throughout their academic journey. Successful applicants will receive: Full tuition fees Textbooks and academic learning materials Accommodation (where required) Monthly student allowance to assist with living expenses This extensive financial support enables recipients to focus on their studies without the burden of major educational costs. Why Apply for the SAIPA Scholarship? Beyond financial assistance, the SAIPA Scholarship provides students with an opportunity to begin their journey toward becoming qualified professional accountants. Benefits of the programme include: Complete financial support throughout university studies Access to quality accounting education Opportunity to join one of South Africa s most respected professional accounting communities A pathway toward professional accountancy qualifications Reduced financial stress, allowing students to concentrate on academic success Recognition through a prestigious national scholarship programme The scholarship also contributes to developing the next generation of accounting professionals who will strengthen South Africa s financial and business sectors. Fields of Study Covered The scholarship supports students pursuing: Bachelor of Commerce (BCom) in Accounting Equivalent Accounting qualifications offered by accredited public universities Applicants must intend to study full-time . Who Can Apply? Applicants must meet all the minimum eligibility requirements before submitting an application. You must: Be a South African citizen . Be currently in Matric or have completed Matric within the past two years . Intend to study full-time . Plan to enroll for a BCom Accounting degree (or equivalent) . Be accepted or intend to enroll at a recognized and accredited public university in South Africa . Have achieved at least 70% in both Mathematics and English . Demonstrate financial need with a household income not exceeding: R350,000 per year , or R600,000 per year for students with disabilities . Failure to meet any of the above requirements may result in the application not being considered. Selection Process Eligible applicants will undergo a competitive selection process consisting of: Initial eligibility screening. A qualifying examination. An interview conducted by a SAIPA selection panel. Successful applicants will be notified by: 23 January 2026 Applicants who do not receive communication by this date should consider their applications unsuccessful. Required Supporting Documents Applicants are not required to complete a formal application form. Instead, eligible candidates should submit an email application together with clear copies of the following compulsory documents: Certified copy of South African ID document. Certified copy of latest Matric results (if currently in Matric). Certified copy of Matric certificate or final statement of results (if Matric has already been completed). Proof of acceptance from an accredited South African public university. Certified copies of parents or guardians identity documents. Recommendation letters supporting the application. A 250-word motivational letter explaining why you should be awarded the scholarship and how it will benefit your educational journey. Incomplete applications or missing documentation may lead to disqualification. How to Apply Eligible applicants should submit their application via email. Email Address: scholarship@saipa.co.za Email Subject Line: SAIPA Scholarship 2026 There is no application form to complete. Applicants only need to email the required supporting documents. Why This Scholarship Matters The rising cost of university education continues to prevent many talented students from pursuing careers in accounting. Through this scholarship, SAIPA is investing in future accounting professionals by providing comprehensive financial support and expanding access to higher education for deserving students from financially disadvantaged households. The programme also strengthens the accounting profession by nurturing skilled graduates who will contribute to South Africa s economic development. For students passionate about accounting, finance, auditing, taxation, and business, this scholarship represents a valuable opportunity to begin a rewarding professional career without the burden of tuition and living expenses. Deadline Application Deadline: 15 January 2026 Late applications will not be accepted. Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare their supporting documents early and submit their applications well before the closing date. Click here for more and to apply Click here for more global scholarship opportunities The post Fully Funded SAIPA Scholarship Programme 2026: Study BCom Accounting in South Africa with Full Tuition, Accommodation, Books, and Monthly Allowance appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNDP Timbuktoo Launchpad 2026: Applications Open for African CreativeTech Startups to Join a Pan-African Incubation Programme
summary: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) has officially opened applications for the first cohort of the Timbuktoo Launchpad 2026 , a pan-African incubation programme designed to empower innovative CreativeTech startups across the continent. Hosted through the Timbuktoo Creatives Hub in South Africa, this initiative aims to identify, nurture, and accelerate startups developing technology-driven solutions for Africa s rapidly growing creative economy. Selected startups will gain access to structured incubation, expert mentorship, digital tools, investor networks, and opportunities to scale their businesses across Africa. If your startup is transforming industries such as gaming, animation, film, music, digital media, immersive technologies, creative artificial intelligence (AI), design, or other creative sectors, this programme provides an exceptional opportunity to accelerate your growth and connect with one of Africa s largest innovation ecosystems. About the Timbuktoo Launchpad Timbuktoo Launchpad is part of UNDP s flagship Timbuktoo initiative , a continent-wide platform established to position Africa as a global innovation hub by supporting entrepreneurs with the resources they need to build impactful and scalable businesses. The programme connects startups with accredited incubators, experienced mentors, investors, ecosystem partners, and learning platforms that provide practical guidance throughout their entrepreneurial journey. The first incubation cohort will begin in: September 2026 Applications are accepted on a rolling basis , with new cohorts starting every three months. Why the Creative Economy Matters Africa s creative economy is one of the continent s fastest-growing sectors, fueled by: Rapid digital transformation Increased internet connectivity A growing youth population Rising demand for digital content Expansion of technology-enabled creative businesses Across Africa, entrepreneurs are creating innovative solutions in digital entertainment, education, culture, media, and creative technologies. However, many startups continue to encounter significant challenges, including: Limited access to business mentorship Difficulty attracting investment Limited networking opportunities Lack of structured incubation support Challenges scaling into regional markets The Timbuktoo Launchpad seeks to bridge these gaps by providing startups with the knowledge, resources, and networks required to grow sustainable businesses. What is the Timbuktoo Creatives Hub? The Timbuktoo Creatives Hub , hosted in South Africa, serves as a continental platform dedicated to supporting innovation within Africa s creative industries. The hub connects entrepreneurs with: Industry experts Investors Innovation hubs Incubators Business mentors Strategic partners Markets across Africa Its mission is to strengthen Africa s creative economy while helping startups become globally competitive businesses. Programme Benefits Selected startups will receive comprehensive business support designed to improve growth, innovation, and investment readiness. Benefits include: Structured Incubation Participants will join professionally managed incubation programmes delivered by leading African incubators within the Timbuktoo ecosystem. These programmes help founders strengthen: Business strategy Product development Customer acquisition Market expansion Business operations Leadership capacity Expert Mentorship Startups will receive guidance from: Experienced entrepreneurs Industry professionals Business advisors Innovation experts Startup mentors Mentorship focuses on helping founders overcome common startup challenges while building scalable businesses. Pan-African Innovation Network Participants become part of a growing ecosystem that includes: Startup founders Innovation hubs Investors Corporate partners Accelerators Incubators Development organizations This network creates opportunities for collaboration, partnerships, and long-term business growth across Africa. Access to the Seedstars SIGMA Platform Selected startups will receive access to the Seedstars SIGMA platform , which powers the Timbuktoo Launchpad experience. The platform provides: AI-powered startup guidance Mentor matching Learning resources Business development tools Progress tracking Investment readiness support These digital resources help founders build stronger businesses while preparing for future fundraising opportunities. Investment Readiness The programme prepares startups to engage investors through: Pitch deck development Investor engagement coaching Business refinement Growth planning Fundraising preparation Demo Days and Ecosystem Events Participants may also receive opportunities to: Present their businesses during demo days Participate in innovation showcases Join startup ecosystem events across Africa Meet investors and ecosystem partners Pathways to Further Growth High-performing startups may gain access to: Acceleration programmes Early-stage investment opportunities Expanded regional partnerships Continued ecosystem support Who Should Apply? UNDP encourages applications from startups that are building innovative technology solutions for Africa s creative industries. Eligible startups should: Be based in Africa or developing products for African markets. Have a working prototype or Minimum Viable Product (MVP). Have a committed founding team. Build technology-enabled solutions. Demonstrate strong growth potential and scalability. CreativeTech Sectors Eligible Applications are welcome from startups working across a wide range of creative industries, including: Gaming Animation Film Music Digital Media Creative AI Creator Economy Platforms Design Immersive Technologies Cultural Industries Digital Content Creation Interactive Media Other technology-enabled creative innovations If your startup combines creativity with technology to solve real-world problems or create new market opportunities, this programme could be an ideal fit. Priority Applicants While applications are open to eligible startups across Africa, UNDP particularly encourages applications from: Women-led startups Youth entrepreneurs Founders from underserved communities This reflects the programme s commitment to promoting inclusive innovation and expanding opportunities for diverse entrepreneurs. About Timbuktoo Timbuktoo is UNDP s flagship innovation initiative designed to establish Africa as a global leader in entrepreneurship and innovation. Its ambitious long-term goals include: Supporting 10,000 startups Scaling 1,000 high-growth ventures Mobilizing US$1 billion in investment Creating transformative economic opportunities throughout Africa The initiative brings together innovation hubs, universities, investors, entrepreneurs, and ecosystem partners to strengthen Africa s startup ecosystem and unlock the continent s innovation potential. Why You Should Apply Joining the Timbuktoo Launchpad offers far more than traditional incubation support. It provides founders with access to one of Africa s most comprehensive startup ecosystems, connecting them with experienced mentors, investors, strategic partners, and business development resources. Whether you re building the next breakthrough gaming platform, revolutionizing digital media, advancing creative AI, or transforming Africa s film, music, or animation industries, this programme can help accelerate your journey from startup to scalable business. If you re ready to grow your CreativeTech venture and become part of a pan-African network of innovators, now is the perfect time to apply. Deadline Applications are open on a rolling basis. The first incubation cohort begins in September 2026 , with additional cohorts starting every three months. Early applications are strongly encouraged.
