title: NYC Civic Corps 2026–2027 Applications Open: Earn a $24,500 Stipend, Gain Professional Experience, and Make a Lasting Community Impact in New York City
summary: If you are looking for a meaningful opportunity after graduation or are considering a career transition into public service, community engagement, or nonprofit leadership, the NYC Civic Corps 2026–2027 program offers an exceptional opportunity to gain practical experience while making a measurable difference in communities across New York City. Applications are now open for the 10-month, full-time national service program, which allows participants to work with City agencies and nonprofit organizations on projects that improve the lives of New Yorkers. Members receive hands-on professional experience, leadership development, networking opportunities, and financial support throughout their service year. Whether your passion lies in community outreach, education, public health, environmental sustainability, volunteer management, youth development, or program administration, NYC Civic Corps provides a pathway to develop valuable career skills while contributing to meaningful public service. Program Overview NYC Civic Corps is a full-time national service program administered through NYC Service. Members are placed in City agencies and nonprofit organizations throughout New York City where they support initiatives that strengthen neighborhoods, improve access to essential services, and build stronger communities. The 2026–2027 cohort will serve from: September 2026 – June 2027 Throughout the program, members work approximately 35 hours per week , combining practical service with professional development and leadership training. The program welcomes: Recent college graduates Individuals changing careers Emerging professionals interested in nonprofit work Future public service leaders Community advocates Individuals passionate about civic engagement Why Join NYC Civic Corps? NYC Civic Corps is much more than a traditional internship or volunteer experience. Members gain: Real-world professional experience Leadership development Government and nonprofit work experience Access to professional networking opportunities Career coaching and mentoring Practical project management experience Community engagement expertise Research and program development skills Public speaking and communication experience Many alumni have successfully transitioned into long-term careers in public service, government agencies, nonprofit organizations, education, and community leadership. According to the program: Approximately 78% of employed alumni now work in public service careers. Example Opportunity: Community Outreach and Engagement One featured placement is with the NYC Commission on Gender Equity , where members support outreach and engagement initiatives by: Planning public events Organizing community engagement activities Developing educational curriculum Conducting policy and community research Building relationships with New Yorkers Supporting civic participation Expanding awareness of gender equity initiatives This placement allows participants to gain firsthand experience working within city government while serving diverse communities. Areas Where Members Serve NYC Civic Corps members contribute to projects across many sectors. These include: Community Development Food Access Environment and Sustainability Public Health Youth Development Education Arts and Culture Urban Planning Civic Engagement Emergency Response Volunteer Coordination Social Services Community Outreach Members have opportunities to work on projects such as: Managing volunteers at food pantries Supporting environmental cleanup initiatives Mentoring young people Assisting during city emergencies Connecting underserved communities to healthcare resources Expanding civic participation Supporting educational initiatives Types of Positions Available Participants may serve in several different professional roles depending on their interests and host organization. 1. Community Outreach Responsibilities may include: Conducting workshops Community engagement Building partnerships Providing public resources Creating outreach campaigns Developing communication materials 2. Volunteer Management Members may: Recruit volunteers Train community volunteers Coordinate volunteer events Manage volunteer databases Lead service projects Strengthen nonprofit capacity 3. Program Development Responsibilities include: Research Data collection Curriculum development Program evaluation Process improvement Operational support Financial Benefits Participants receive an attractive benefits package during their service year. Benefits include: $24,500 pre-tax living stipend $5,176 Education Award OMNY transportation benefits Professional development workshops Monthly leadership training Networking opportunities One-on-one professional support 15 paid time off (PTO) days Loan forbearance (where eligible) Health coverage Childcare assistance (if eligible) SNAP benefits These benefits make the program financially accessible while allowing members to focus on professional growth and community impact. Professional Development One of the strongest aspects of NYC Civic Corps is the extensive professional development offered throughout the year. Participants receive: Leadership training Career coaching Networking events Professional mentorship Skills development workshops Public service career preparation Resume-building experience Government workplace exposure By the end of the program, members leave with practical work experience that strengthens future employment opportunities. Program Impact NYC Civic Corps has created significant impact across New York City. Recent program achievements include: More than 150 members receiving training, networking opportunities, and personalized support. More than 88,000 New Yorkers served during the current program year. More than 100 City agencies and nonprofit organizations strengthened through member service. Approximately 78% of employed alumni pursuing careers in public service. These outcomes demonstrate the program s commitment to developing future leaders while strengthening local communities. Host Organizations Members serve alongside respected nonprofit organizations and City agencies working across a wide range of sectors. Examples of nonprofit host organizations include: Big Brothers Big Sisters of NYC Bronx River Arts Center Catholic Charities of New York Council on American Islamic Relations – New York Queens Public Library Waterfront Alliance Youth Journalism Coalition GlamourGals Foundation Opus 118 Harlem School of Music Examples of City agency partners include: Administration for Children s Services Civic Engagement Commission Department of Cultural Affairs Department of Sanitation Mayor s Office of Immigrant Affairs Mayor s Office to End Gender-Based Violence Mayor s Office of International Affairs NYC Emergency Management NYC Parks NYC Public Schools Additionally, members may be placed at over 70 other nonprofit organizations and City agencies throughout New York City. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet all eligibility requirements before joining the program. You must: Be 18 years of age or older . Have earned a high school diploma or equivalent before the program begins. Be a U.S. citizen, U.S. national, or lawful permanent resident . Pass the required National Service Criminal History Check . Be available to serve full-time (35 hours per week) from September 2026 through June 2027. Application Process The application process consists of four steps. Step 1: Apply Explore available positions. Select three preferred opportunities. Submit your application. Step 2: Interview Complete an interview with NYC Service. Interview with prospective host organizations. Step 3: Receive an Offer Accept your preferred placement. Complete onboarding requirements. Step 4: Begin Service Start serving your assigned organization while gaining valuable professional experience and making a positive impact across New York City. Alumni Success Stories Many former NYC Civic Corps members describe the experience as transformative. Alumni have gone on to careers in: Government Public administration Community engagement Volunteer management Education Public health Food security Nonprofit leadership Several alumni credit the program with helping them discover career paths they had never previously considered while developing leadership skills that continue to benefit their professional lives. Why You Should Apply NYC Civic Corps offers far more than a stipend. It provides participants with practical experience, leadership training, valuable professional connections, and the opportunity to create meaningful social impact. If you are passionate about serving communities, strengthening public institutions, and building a rewarding career in public service or the nonprofit sector, this program is an outstanding opportunity to launch your career while making a real difference. Click here for more and to apply Deadline No application deadline has been specified in the provided information. Applications are currently open. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible before available positions are filled For more global internship opportunities, Click here The post NYC Civic Corps 2026–2027 Applications Open: Earn a $24,500 Stipend, Gain Professional Experience, and Make a Lasting Community Impact in New York City appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: IOM Policy Officer (P-2) Jobs 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland: Apply to Help Shape Global Migration Policy
summary: The International Organization for Migration (IOM) , the leading United Nations agency on migration, is inviting qualified professionals to apply for the position of Policy Officer (P-2) at its Headquarters in Geneva, Switzerland . This is an outstanding opportunity for professionals passionate about migration policy, governance, international cooperation, and evidence-based policymaking to contribute to global efforts that promote safe, orderly, and humane migration. Working within IOM s Migration Policy Division, the successful candidate will help support governments by providing strategic policy advice, conducting policy analysis, strengthening migration governance, and contributing to the implementation of the Migration Governance Indicators (MGI) and the IOM Policy Agenda . Application Deadline: July 14, 2026 About the International Organization for Migration (IOM) Established in 1951 , the International Organization for Migration (IOM) is the leading intergovernmental organization working on migration issues worldwide and is a Related Organization of the United Nations. IOM works closely with governments, international organizations, civil society, and communities to promote humane and orderly migration that benefits both migrants and societies. The organization supports countries by saving lives, protecting people on the move, responding to displacement, facilitating pathways for regular migration, and providing technical expertise and policy guidance. Operating in countries across the globe, IOM is committed to creating an inclusive, respectful, and supportive workplace where employees can contribute to meaningful international development and humanitarian efforts. About the Policy Officer (P-2) Position The Policy Officer will work under the supervision of the Chief of the Migration Policy Division and the Senior Policy Development and Coordination Officer. The role focuses on supporting governments and IOM offices worldwide through evidence-based migration policy development, strategic analysis, institutional coordination, and policy implementation. The successful candidate will collaborate with IOM Headquarters, Regional Offices, Country Offices, governments, and international partners to strengthen migration governance and institutional policy capacity. Key Responsibilities The selected Policy Officer will be responsible for: Supporting migration policy advisory services for IOM Country and Regional Offices. Conducting policy analysis and coordinating expert inputs for migration policy development. Supporting the implementation of the IOM Policy Agenda. Coordinating priority migration policy processes with governments and regional offices. Developing policy intelligence, strategic analysis, foresight, and evidence-based analytical products. Supporting the implementation of the Migration Governance Indicators (MGI) and related data initiatives. Coordinating national and local migration governance assessments. Producing analytical reports and promoting evidence-informed migration policymaking. Drafting high-quality policy papers, briefing notes, speeches, technical guidance, and strategic reports. Promoting collaboration across IOM Headquarters, Regional Offices, and Country Offices. Supporting capacity-building initiatives, workshops, technical assistance, and government engagement. Contributing to proposal development, donor reporting, partnership building, and fundraising opportunities. Participating in international meetings, workshops, and technical consultations. Required Qualifications Applicants should possess one of the following: A Master s degree in Political Science, International Relations, Social Sciences, Law, or a related field with at least two years of relevant professional experience , or A Bachelor s degree in one of the above disciplines with at least four years of relevant professional experience . Degrees must be obtained from accredited institutions recognized by the UNESCO World Higher Education Database. Professional Experience Ideal candidates should demonstrate experience in: Migration policy development. Migration governance. Public policy analysis. Policy advisory services. Government engagement. Technical assistance. Capacity-building initiatives. Policy dialogues. Programme or project management. Partnership development. Experience working at national, regional, or international levels is highly desirable. Required Skills Candidates should have: Strong analytical and strategic thinking skills. Excellent organizational and project management abilities. Outstanding English drafting and report-writing skills. Experience preparing policy papers and technical documents. Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills. Ability to build partnerships across multicultural environments. Strong teamwork and collaboration skills. Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and the effective use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools. Knowledge of migration governance frameworks, migration policy processes, and evidence-based policymaking. Experience with Migration Governance Indicators (MGI) or similar analytical tools is considered an advantage. Language Requirements Applicants must demonstrate: Fluency in English (written and spoken). Working knowledge of French and/or Spanish is considered an advantage. Why Work at IOM? Joining IOM provides professionals with an opportunity to: Contribute to global migration governance. Influence evidence-based international migration policy. Work alongside governments and international organizations. Collaborate with experts from around the world. Support initiatives that improve migration management and protect vulnerable populations. Build an international career within the United Nations system. Gain exposure to high-level policy development and international cooperation. Duty Station The successful candidate will be based at: Geneva, Switzerland Important Information This vacancy forms part of IOM s competitive mobility exercise and is open for applications from internal and internal-equivalent candidates only . Applicants should carefully review the eligibility requirements before applying. IOM is an equal opportunity employer that promotes diversity, inclusion, integrity, professionalism, accountability, and respect in the workplace. The organization maintains a zero-tolerance policy against discrimination, harassment, exploitation, abuse, and fraud throughout its recruitment process. Importantly, IOM does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process . Application Deadline Applications must be submitted no later than: July 14, 2026 Late applications will not be accepted. How to Apply Interested candidates should visit the official IOM Careers portal to review the complete vacancy announcement, eligibility requirements, and application procedures. Click here for more and to apply Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues. Click here for more global job opportunities The post IOM Policy Officer (P-2) Jobs 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland: Apply to Help Shape Global Migration Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM 2026: Fully Funded International Startup Accelerator for Young Social Entrepreneurs in Latin America
summary: Applications are now open for the Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM 2026 , an exciting international entrepreneurship programme designed to empower young impact entrepreneurs across Latin America. Implemented under the EU-LAC Social Accelerator Project , the bootcamp provides practical training, international exposure, peer networking, expert coaching, and investment readiness support for startups addressing social and environmental challenges. The programme is implemented by the International Trade Centre (ITC) in partnership with a consortium comprising: CAINCO Caribbean Export Development Agency Central American Bank for Economic Integration (CACB) Tecnalia Tecnológico de Monterrey The programme supports young entrepreneurs developing innovative businesses that contribute to the green, digital, and inclusive transition in Latin America, in alignment with the European Union s Global Gateway Agenda . Delivered entirely in Spanish , the Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM offers founders an opportunity to strengthen their businesses, develop practical entrepreneurial skills, expand professional networks, and prepare for international growth opportunities. About the Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM The Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM is a comprehensive accelerator programme created specifically for entrepreneurs who are already building businesses and need practical tools rather than classroom-based theory. Unlike traditional entrepreneurship courses, the programme combines: Live expert-led masterclasses Self-paced digital learning Practical business challenges Peer-to-peer learning Networking opportunities Personalized coaching International exposure The bootcamp is community-driven and designed to produce measurable progress in participants businesses throughout the programme. Programme Objectives The programme seeks to: Strengthen impact-driven startups across Latin America Support businesses contributing to green, digital, and inclusive economies Prepare entrepreneurs for investment and international expansion Build stronger entrepreneurial ecosystems Promote innovation aligned with the European Union Global Gateway priorities Connect founders with international partners and investors Programme Structure The Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM consists of two comprehensive phases . Phase One: Bootcamp Phase Programme Period: 24 August 2026 – 16 December 2026 This first phase combines practical entrepreneurship education with continuous collaboration and business development. Participants will take part in: Live masterclasses Impact Circles Coffee Roulettes Self-paced online learning Practical assignments Business development exercises Peer collaboration sessions At the end of the Bootcamp Phase, participants will compete in an internal pitch competition. Following this competition, the Top 8 entrepreneurs will be selected to advance to the next stage. Phase Two: High Performance Phase Programme Period: January – April 2027 The High Performance Phase provides deeper support for the selected entrepreneurs through: Individual coaching Personalized mentoring International visibility Investor exposure Business acceleration Opportunities to pitch before: European Union delegations Programme partners Investors Business stakeholders What Participants Will Learn The curriculum covers essential entrepreneurial competencies required to build sustainable impact businesses. Business Foundations Participants will strengthen: Business strategy Growth planning Sustainable business models Operational development Marketing Founders will learn: Marketing strategy Customer engagement Brand positioning Market expansion Business Model Canvas Design Thinking Sessions focus on: Business model development Innovation Customer-centred product design Problem-solving methodologies Artificial Intelligence Tools Participants will explore: AI applications for startups Productivity tools Digital transformation Business automation ESG and Responsible Business Experts will introduce: Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) principles Responsible business practices Sustainable entrepreneurship These sessions are delivered in collaboration with: EY Legal Frameworks for ESG Participants will receive legal guidance on: ESG compliance Responsible business governance International legal considerations These sessions are delivered with: Sidley Standards and Compliance Training includes: International business standards Market readiness Quality frameworks Pitch Training Entrepreneurs will develop: Investor pitching skills Presentation techniques Storytelling Business communication Vibe Coding and No-Code Tools Founders will gain practical exposure to: No-code development Digital product creation Technology tools for entrepreneurs Additional Digital Courses In addition to live sessions, participants complete two self-paced online courses. Investment Readiness This course helps entrepreneurs: Prepare for fundraising Improve investor communication Understand financing opportunities Social Impact Participants learn how to: Measure social impact Evaluate environmental outcomes Communicate impact effectively Build stronger impact strategies Community-Based Learning Activities One of the programme s distinguishing features is its collaborative learning environment. Impact Circles Participants join regional peer-learning groups where they: Share business progress Discuss challenges Receive feedback Apply lessons learned Coffee Roulettes These one-on-one networking sessions allow entrepreneurs to: Meet fellow founders Exchange experiences Explore collaborations Build professional relationships Practical Challenges Throughout the programme, founders complete practical business exercises that reinforce learning through implementation. Benefits of the Programme Selected entrepreneurs will receive numerous benefits. 1. Expert-Led Entrepreneurship Training Access to practical business education delivered by international experts. 2. Personalized Coaching Top-performing entrepreneurs receive tailored business coaching. 3. International Exposure Selected participants gain opportunities to present their businesses before: European Union representatives Programme partners Investors International stakeholders 4. Investment Readiness Support Participants strengthen their ability to attract future funding. 5. Networking Opportunities Connect with: Fellow entrepreneurs Industry experts Investors International organizations 6. Community Membership Every participant joins the Ye! Community , a global entrepreneurial network that provides: Networking opportunities Future business opportunities Continued engagement after the programme Peer support 7. Practical Business Growth The programme focuses on helping entrepreneurs achieve measurable improvements in: Business performance Market readiness Operational systems Growth strategies Priority Themes The programme prioritizes startups working in sectors aligned with the European Union Global Gateway investment priorities in Latin America, including: Renewable energy Green hydrogen Sustainable agriculture Forestry Biodiversity Green economy Digital innovation Inclusive development Founders building solutions connected to flagship projects in their respective countries are especially encouraged to apply. Who Should Apply? The Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM is ideal for: Social entrepreneurs Environmental innovators Startup founders Green business owners Technology entrepreneurs Young business leaders Early-stage startups seeking growth Eligibility Criteria Applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements: At least one co-founder must be younger than 35 years old. The company must be legally registered, active, and operate as a for-profit business. The business must already exist; the programme is not intended for business ideas alone. The startup must generate measurable social and/or environmental impact. The company must be based in: Argentina Colombia El Salvador Mexico Applicants must be available to dedicate approximately two hours per week to programme activities. Application Requirements Before beginning the application, applicants should prepare: Personal identification document Company registration certificate (where applicable) Basic company information Details about: Business model Business activities Social or environmental impact Applicants should note: The application takes approximately 20–30 minutes to complete. The application must be completed in one sitting . Applicants may download a sample list of questions before beginning the application. How to Apply Applicants should: Join the Ye! Community . Complete the Online Application Form . Submit all requested information before the application deadline. Programme Timeline Applications Open: Currently Open Application Deadline: 19 July 2026 Programme Starts: 24 August 2026 Bootcamp Phase: 24 August – 16 December 2026 Pitch Competition: December 2026 High Performance Phase: January – April 2027 Contact Information For programme or application questions, applicants may contact: Email: youthandtrade@intracen.org Why You Should Apply The Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM is much more than a startup training programme. It provides founders with: International mentorship Practical entrepreneurial education Peer collaboration Business acceleration Investment readiness preparation Exposure to investors Access to the European Union innovation ecosystem Membership in a global entrepreneurial network Whether you are scaling a climate startup, building a digital solution, or creating a socially impactful enterprise, this programme offers valuable resources to help accelerate your business journey. Visit HERE to learn more about the Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM 2026. Conclusion The Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM 2026 represents one of the most comprehensive entrepreneurship acceleration opportunities currently available for young founders in Latin America. By combining expert-led training, practical business development, investment readiness, peer collaboration, personalized coaching, and international exposure, the programme equips entrepreneurs with the knowledge, networks, and confidence needed to grow sustainable businesses that address pressing social and environmental challenges. If you are a young entrepreneur in Argentina, Colombia, El Salvador, or Mexico leading an innovative business with measurable impact, this programme provides a unique opportunity to strengthen your venture, connect with global partners, and position your startup for future investment and international expansion. Interested founders should prepare their application materials and submit their applications before 19 July 2026 to be considered for this highly competitive programme. Discover more global Fellowship and Accelerator programs on OFY. The post Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM 2026: Fully Funded International Startup Accelerator for Young Social Entrepreneurs in Latin America appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNICEF Knowledge Management Analyst Job Opportunity: Advance Digital Safety, Child Rights, and Cybersecurity Through Global Knowledge Leadership
summary: UNICEF is seeking a talented and highly motivated Knowledge Management Analyst to join its Digital Impact Division in an exciting role focused on strengthening digital safety, cybersecurity awareness, knowledge management, advocacy, research, and child rights in the digital age. This position offers professionals an opportunity to contribute to UNICEF s growing Digital Guardrails Agenda , helping develop innovative knowledge products, learning programmes, advocacy campaigns, research outputs, and strategic partnerships that promote safe, secure, and rights-respecting digital ecosystems for children worldwide. If you are passionate about digital transformation, cybersecurity, knowledge management, international development, and protecting children s rights online, this position provides a unique opportunity to work alongside international experts while making a global impact. About the UNICEF Knowledge Management Analyst Position The Knowledge Management Analyst is far more than a traditional training or communications role. Rather than focusing solely on developing educational materials, the successful candidate will help UNICEF connect, organize, translate, and scale knowledge across multiple technical and policy areas. Working under the supervision of the Knowledge Management Officer , the successful candidate will support initiatives that combine: Knowledge management Information security awareness Cybersecurity education Research and evidence generation Digital policy Child rights advocacy Behaviour change communication International partnerships Learning programme development The position bridges technical expertise and public communication by transforming complex digital governance and cybersecurity concepts into practical tools, engaging learning experiences, compelling advocacy materials, and evidence-based policy resources. Why This Opportunity Matters Digital technologies continue transforming how children learn, communicate, and access services. At the same time, emerging digital risks—including cybersecurity threats, data privacy concerns, misinformation, and online harms—require coordinated global responses. UNICEF s Digital Guardrails initiative seeks to strengthen digital ecosystems that protect children s rights while promoting responsible innovation. As a Knowledge Management Analyst, you will contribute directly to these efforts by helping UNICEF: Build stronger cybersecurity awareness among staff. Develop evidence-based digital policies. Produce practical knowledge resources. Strengthen advocacy for child rights in digital environments. Support governments and international partners. Improve digital resilience across UNICEF programmes. Purpose of the Role The position supports the Digital Impact Division by designing and implementing integrated knowledge management and learning solutions that strengthen UNICEF s Digital Guardrails agenda. The role contributes to four major areas: Strengthening information security awareness and behavioural change. Supporting advocacy and communication around digital safety. Advancing research on cybersecurity, digital governance, and child rights. Enhancing collaboration across UNICEF, governments, UN agencies, and international partners. Key Responsibilities 1. Learning and Information Security Awareness The successful candidate will: Design information security awareness programmes. Develop mandatory and targeted learning initiatives. Support multilingual learning delivery. Improve cybersecurity awareness across UNICEF. Introduce innovative learning experiences that encourage behavioural change. Continuously evaluate and improve learning programmes. 2. Knowledge Products and Communications The role involves producing high-quality communication resources, including: Guidance documents Policy briefs Toolkits Communication assets Learning materials Educational resources The analyst will simplify highly technical concepts into accessible content suitable for a wide range of audiences. 3. Advocacy and Strategic Partnerships The position contributes to UNICEF s advocacy efforts by: Developing key messages. Supporting advocacy campaigns. Preparing briefing documents. Creating materials for conferences and events. Producing resources for governments and partners. Supporting collaboration with: UN agencies Government institutions Technical experts International stakeholders 4. Research and Knowledge Generation Research responsibilities include: Conducting literature reviews. Synthesizing research findings. Drafting analytical reports. Producing evidence-based policy narratives. Supporting research on: Cybersecurity Data governance Digital risks Child online protection Digital safeguards 5. Data Monitoring and Programme Improvement The analyst will also support programme monitoring through: Training completion analysis. Campaign engagement tracking. Dashboard development. Reporting and visualization. Performance monitoring. Strategic decision support. Expected Deliverables Throughout the assignment, the Knowledge Management Analyst will contribute to: Quarterly Deliverables Improved learning and awareness initiatives. Information security training. Phishing simulation programmes. Information Security Awareness bulletins. Communication materials. Knowledge products. Advocacy campaigns. Programme dashboards. Monitoring reports. Ongoing Deliverables Literature reviews. Research reports. Policy briefs. Partnership materials. Event resources. Advocacy messaging. Strategic analytical outputs. Education Requirements Applicants must possess: Required A bachelor s degree in: Information Management Digital Studies Computer Science International Development Human Rights Or another closely related field. Preferred A Master s degree or equivalent qualification in a related discipline. Professional Experience Applicants should have a minimum of two years of relevant experience demonstrating expertise in one or more of the following: Cybersecurity Digital governance Digital policy Child rights Human rights Research Knowledge management Communications Candidates should also demonstrate experience in: Data analysis Data visualization Research synthesis Knowledge product development Learning content creation Communication material development Desired Skills UNICEF is particularly interested in candidates who demonstrate: Intellectual curiosity. Strong analytical thinking. Excellent writing abilities. Initiative and independence. Creativity. Adaptability. Strong collaboration skills. Interdisciplinary thinking. Experience using AI tools for research or prototyping. Familiarity with e-learning platforms. Experience with digital communication tools. Knowledge of visual content creation tools. Who Is the Ideal Candidate? The organization is seeking professionals who are: Curious and eager to learn. Comfortable working across multiple disciplines. Strong problem-solvers. Able to manage changing priorities. Creative communicators. Passionate about child rights. Interested in digital transformation. Motivated by global impact. Why Join UNICEF? Working with UNICEF provides an opportunity to: Contribute to global digital transformation initiatives. Protect children s rights in digital spaces. Collaborate with international experts. Work across governments and UN agencies. Develop innovative knowledge products. Influence digital policy and cybersecurity awareness. Build expertise in research, advocacy, learning, and international development. Application Information Interested applicants should prepare a professional application highlighting: Relevant education. Professional experience. Research and analytical skills. Knowledge management experience. Communication abilities. Interest in digital transformation and child rights. Experience with cybersecurity, AI tools, or digital policy where applicable. Apply Here Application Deadline: Not specified in the announcement. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible once the official vacancy is available.
